methods.description: This method involves measuring sedimentary structures to determine flow directions during sedimentation. The analysis helps in identifying the vector of the sediment source and associating paleocurrent sets with depositional environments or paleoflow directions.

steps.description: Paleoflow structures -trough and planar cross bedding-were measured from 9 beds of sandstone beds along the stratigraphic section.
steps.searchable_summary: measure paleoflow structures from sandstone beds

steps.description: These data were corrected for the structural attitude of the beds by using the software Stereonet 9.8.3.
steps.searchable_summary: correct data for structural attitude using Stereonet

steps.description: The results were plotted in rose diagrams to show their preferential directions.
Different paleocurrent sets along the stratigraphic section were defined based on changes in direction patterns and were associated with depositional environments or/and paleoflow directions.
steps.searchable_summary: define paleocurrent sets based on direction patterns

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the normalized percentage distribution of heavy minerals in samples to understand changes in depositional environments or source area composition.

steps.description: The normalized percentage distribution of heavy minerals was obtained for ten samples that were also normalized without mica.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain normalized percentage distribution of heavy minerals

steps.description: Analyze the distribution of minerals to identify changes in depositional environment or source area composition.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze mineral distribution for environmental changes
